title: [low pri] [tests only] imgtype: incomplete diagnostic when wrong expected-manifest-type
body:
`imgtype` is emitting an incomplete diagnostic when the expected image type is wrong:
```
# cid=$(./buildah from scratch)

#  ./buildah commit --format oci $cid scratch-image-oci
[...]

# ./imgtype --expected-manifest-type application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json  scratch-image-oci
ERRO[0000] expected manifest type "application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json" in "scratch-image-oci", got ""
```
Note the `got ""` at far right. I expected `got "application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"`

The converse test (for correct manifest type) works fine and is silent as expected:
```
# ./imgtype --expected-manifest-type application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json scratch-image-oci
```
Also, when the image type is docker, the diagnostic includes the actual type:
```
# ./buildah commit --format docker $cid scratch-image-docker
[...]

# ./imgtype --expected-manifest-type application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json scratch-image-docker
ERRO[0000] expected config type "application/vnd.oci.image.config.v1+json" in "scratch-image-docker", got "application/vnd.docker.container.image.v1+json"
```
(likely because they are entirely different code paths. Note also that this code path says `unexpected config type`, whereas the other says `unexpected manitest type`. Probably not a critical inconsistency).
